title: Redspring benchmarks open-source VLM OCR across 100,000 pages
friendlyTitle: A 100,000-page OCR test shows why your own documents matter
summary: Redspring tested open-source vision-language models for OCR on a 100,000-page workload using Modal, comparing cost, throughput, and output quality. The team found that self-hosted OCR could be cheaper and easier than expected, but that public benchmarks did not reliably predict the best model for its own documents.
friendlySummary: Redspring tested open-source AI OCR models on a huge document job and found that cost, speed, and quality depend heavily on the exact documents.
whyItMatters: Document pipelines are central to RAG, compliance, and agent workflows. The practical lesson is to benchmark on your own messy documents, measure silent hallucinations, and choose GPUs based on wall-clock requirements as well as price per second.
friendlyWhyItMatters: If you use AI to read PDFs or scans, do not pick a model only from public rankings. Test it on your own files first.
